# Alkimi Configurator — Agent Contract

> Audience: AI/LLM agents (internal Alkimi staff agents, and external agents acting on behalf of clients) that need to read or drive this app.
> Source of truth: the live Base44 entity schemas. This document mirrors them; if the two diverge, the live schema wins.

## App at a glance

- **What it is:** Internal quoting tool for Alkimi (LED tape-light fixtures).
- **Stack:** Vite + React + Tailwind + shadcn + React Query, talking to a Base44 backend.
- **Base44 app id:** `698fc81203f85a20f281d9dc`
- **Prod URL:** `https://light-calc-pro.base44.app`
- **Auth:** Base44 session token (Google sign-in). Localhost uses a `?access_token=` bootstrap.

## Entities

### Project

Top-level quote container. One project has many `TapeRun`s and one inline `drivers[]` array.

| Field | Type | Required | Enum | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `project_name` | string | yes | — | |
| `customer_name` | string | no | — | |
| `customer_email` | string (email) | no | — | |
| `customer_phone` | string | no | — | |
| `street` / `city` | string | no | — | |
| `state` | string | no | 2-letter US state codes (48 — no DC/PR/VI/GU) | |
| `sector` | string | no | Commercial, Education, Healthcare, Hospitality, Residential, Other | |
| `notes` | string | no | — | |
| `quote_number` | string | no | — | Auto-generated as `QUOTE-NNN` when status flips to `approved`. |
| `status` | string | default `draft` | draft, submitted, approved | |
| `drivers` | array | no | — | `[{id: string, name: string, maxWatts: 60\|96}]` |
| `created_by` | string | system | — | User email (Base44). |

**Row-level security (RLS):** every operation is gated on `created_by == {{user.email}}` OR `user.role == admin`. An agent acting as a user can only see and modify that user's projects.

### TapeRun

A single tape-light run within a project.

| Field | Type | Required | Enum | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `project_id` | string | yes | — | FK to Project. |
| `run_name` | string | no | — | Short label shown in the UI as "Name". |
| `length_feet` | number | yes | — | Snaps to nearest `2.5"` increment in the UI. |
| `tape_output` | string | no | `300lm (3.0w/ft)`, `360lm (3.6w/ft)`, `600lm (6.0w/ft)` | Tape brightness/wattage spec. Default `300lm`. |
| `product_type` | string | no | Tape, Flex | |
| `location` | string | no | — | Free-text room/zone label. |
| `cct` | string | no | `2400k`, `2700k`, `3000k`, `3500k`, `Warm Dim (30k-18k)`, `Tunable White (18k-40k)` | `Tunable White` is reserved/disabled in the UI today. `Warm Dim` forces `tape_output` to `360lm`. |
| `channel_type` | string | no (default `corner`) | corner, surface, none | `recessed` was removed 2026-05. |
| `lens` | string | no | Clear, Frosted | |
| `finish` | string | no | Aluminum, Black, White | |
| `notes` | string | no | — | |
| `driver_group` | string | no | — | References `drivers[].name` on the parent project (e.g. `Driver 1`). |
| `order` | number | no | — | Display order, ascending. |

### Driver (embedded array on Project)

`Project.drivers[]` is the source of truth for which drivers are on a project. Each item:
`{ id: string, name: string, maxWatts: 60 | 96 }`. Pricing comes from `DRIVER_SPECS` (see Pricing).

## Operations

### Read

- `base44.entities.Project.filter({ id })` / `.list()` — RLS-scoped.
- `base44.entities.TapeRun.filter({ project_id })` — return runs sorted by `order`.

### Write

- `base44.entities.Project.create({...})` — `project_name` is the only required field.
- `base44.entities.Project.update(id, {...})` — partial updates allowed.
- `base44.entities.TapeRun.create({ project_id, length_feet, ...optional })`.
- `base44.entities.TapeRun.update(id, {...})`.
- `base44.entities.TapeRun.delete(id)`.
- `base44.entities.Project.delete(id)` — triggers `cascadeDeleteTapeRuns` to remove children.

### Server functions

- `exportProjectPDF({ project_id, data_env? })` — returns a PDF blob.
- `exportProjectCSV({ project_id, data_env? })` — returns CSV text.
- `cascadeDeleteTapeRuns` — wired to Project delete events.

### Status transitions

- `draft` → `submitted`: set `status: "submitted"` via Project.update.
- `submitted` → `approved`: set `status: "approved"`. The UI does not yet expose this transition — it's agent-only today. On approval, generate a quote number with the pattern `QUOTE-NNN` (next index over all existing quote numbers).
- Any → `draft`: revert.

## Pricing model

Single source of truth: **`base44/shared/pricing.js`**. Both the Vite frontend (via `src/components/calculator/constants.jsx`) and the Base44 server export functions import directly from there.

```
TAPE_SPECS  (price_per_foot, watts_per_foot, lumens_per_foot)
  "300lm (3.0w/ft)": $10/ft, 3.0 W/ft, 300 lm/ft
  "360lm (3.6w/ft)": $11/ft, 3.6 W/ft, 360 lm/ft
  "600lm (6.0w/ft)": $12/ft, 6.0 W/ft, 600 lm/ft

CHANNEL_SPECS (price_per_foot, billed in 4-ft sections)
  corner:  $10/ft
  surface: $8/ft
  none:    $0/ft

DRIVER_SPECS (price by maxWatts)
  60W:  $55
  96W:  $65
DEFAULT_DRIVER_MAX_WATTS = 96   // used when project.drivers is empty
DRIVER_LOAD_FACTOR = 0.8        // size watt-derived fallback to 80% capacity

CLIPS_PER_SECTION = 4   // 4 clips per 4-ft section
CLIPS_PER_SET = 12
CLIP_SET_PRICE = $15

SPOOL_LENGTH_FEET ≈ 16.33   // 16'4"; one tape-to-tape connector per spool join
SHIPPING_RATE = 10%         // applied to subtotal
```

**Driver count:** if `project.drivers` is non-empty, exports price the actual configured driver list (one line per driver, mix labelled like `Drivers (60W+96W+96W)`). If empty, count is `ceil(totalWatts / (96 * 0.8))` priced at $65 each.

**Channel cost:** rounded up to whole 4-ft sections per run, then summed.

## Stable UI selectors

Every interactive control an agent should target has a `data-testid` (and a stable `id` where it's an input).

| Selector | Where | What it does |
|---|---|---|
| `[data-testid=project-new]` | sidebar | Open inline new-project create. |
| `[data-testid=project-search]` | sidebar | Search input over projects. |
| `[data-testid=project-list-item][data-project-id=ID]` | sidebar | Select a project. |
| `[data-testid=project-save]` | header | Save project. |
| `[data-testid=project-submit]` | header | Submit project (status → submitted). |
| `[data-testid=project-delete]` | header | Delete project. |
| `[data-testid=project-export]` | header | Open export dropdown. |
| `[data-testid=project-export-pdf]` / `-csv` | export dropdown | Trigger export. |
| `#project_name`, `#customer_name`, `#customer_email`, `#customer_phone`, `#street`, `#city`, `#state`, `#sector` | ProjectForm | Project field inputs. |
| `[data-testid=tape-run-add]` | TapeRunList | Add the currently-composed run. |
| `#new-run-name`, `#new-run-location`, `#new-run-product`, `#new-run-length-feet`, `#new-run-length-inches`, `#new-run-cct`, `#new-run-output`, `#new-run-housing`, `#new-run-lens`, `#new-run-finish`, `#new-run-driver` | TapeRunList add row | Inputs for the new run. |
| `[data-testid=tape-run-row][data-run-id=ID]` | TapeRunList | Existing run row. |
| `[data-testid=tape-run-edit]` / `-save` / `-cancel` / `-delete` | run row | Per-row actions. |
| `[data-testid=tape-run-move-up]` / `-move-down` | run row | Keyboard- and agent-friendly reorder (alternative to drag). |
| `[data-testid=driver-row][data-driver-name=NAME]` | DriverManager | Driver row. |
| `[data-testid=driver-remove]` | driver row | Remove that driver. |

## URL state

- `?project=<id>` — selected project. Deep-linkable. Setting/clearing this via `useSearchParams` is the supported way to switch projects.

## Conventions agents should respect

- **Schema is the contract.** If you're sending fields, they should appear in the entity schema or they'll be silently dropped.
- **Enum values are exact strings.** `"300lm (3.0w/ft)"` is not the same as `"300lm (3w/ft)"`.
- **Driver references are by name.** `TapeRun.driver_group` must equal one of `project.drivers[].name`. If you create a new driver, append it to `project.drivers` before pointing runs at it.
- **`order` is sparse and 0-based.** Always re-sort by `order` ascending when listing runs.
- **Reorder via the move-up/move-down buttons or by writing new `order` values** — don't try to drive the drag-and-drop UI.
- **Status transitions go through Project.update**, not a dedicated endpoint. Generate a `quote_number` when moving to `approved`.

## Known limits (today)

- No public OpenAPI spec — entities are reachable through the Base44 SDK.
- Approve transition has no UI button (agent-only).
- Driver renaming is not exposed in the UI; renaming a driver orphans runs that reference the old name.

These will close over time. When in doubt, read the live entity schemas via `list_entity_schemas` rather than this file.
